content_type: memorial / Wall of Honor tribute card
description: A designed memorial tribute graphic ('Wall of Honor') in portrait format honoring a fallen ICE/legacy officer. The right side shows a softly-lit photograph of red roses in front of a blurred American flag (stars and stripes visible), evoking solemn remembrance, over a dark slate-gray textured background. The upper-left contains an inset framed historical portrait photograph of the honoree: a man in an early/mid-20th-century uniform with a peaked officer's cap and badge, in front of what appears to be a flag backdrop; the image is grainy/low-resolution consistent with an archival photo. Across the lower-left in elegant white serif type is 'Wall of Honor', accompanied by a stylized ICE memorial badge with a blue mourning ribbon (the badge reads 'ICE'). To the right of the badge: 'Special Agent / Anthony Cozza', then a gold horizontal rule, 'End of Watch' (serif), another gold rule, and the date 'Saturday, February 24, 1945'. No suspects or enforcement imagery; this is institutional commemoration. Messaging technique: somber, patriotic memorialization (flag + roses + serif typography + badge with mourning ribbon) building institutional legacy and valorizing the agency's history of sacrifice. Target audience is the public and ICE personnel; intent is reverence, legitimacy-building, and esprit de corps.
notable_text: Wall of Honor. Special Agent Anthony Cozza. End of Watch. Saturday, February 24, 1945.
